summary: The main claim is: The last few years have undoubtedly been years of scaling monolithic very large models.

We need comprehensive interpretability in order to have strong-ish confidence about whether dangerous representations or circuits exist in our model.

The main claim is: Another use case I can think of is using ChatGPT to help me with memory reconsolidation.
Wondering if there's some prompting I could leverage to help me do more memory reconsolidation for some other lingering issues I have.

The main claim is: A 6-month pause is far too weak; the real danger is what happens once AI passes human intelligence, and under anything like current conditions the most likely result of building superintelligence is that everyone on Earth dies.

We should shut frontier AI development down entirely — indefinitely and with international enforcement — because we are nowhere near knowing how to build something far smarter than us that actually cares about humanity.

top_comment.author: Rob Bensinger
top_comment.text: (Meta: The TIME piece is paywalled in some countries, and is plastered with ads, so Eliezer wanted the text mirrored on the MIRI Blog. He also assented to my having the LW admins cross-post this here. This version adds some clarifying notes Eliezer wrote on Twitter regarding the article.)

summary: The main claim is: However, many of these people might not have a sufficient “toolbox” or research experience to have much marginal impact in short timelines worlds.
However, I suspect most aspiring AI safety researchers without research experience should have different plans for different AGI timeline scenarios.

The main claim is: OpenAI's alignment plan requires roughly six distinct steps to all succeed — build alignment-research assistants, actually use them while slowing capabilities, evaluate their proposals, and more — and if any step fails the plan won't avert catastrophe.
The plan is most underspecified on its hardest steps, especially how OpenAI would slow capabilities research and evaluate alignment strategies proposed by AI assistants.
The main claim is: AI Safety Technical group post examples( 1 ).
These groups are Strategy, Governance, Technical and Movement Building.
